candidates.name: Henry McMaster
candidates.notes: South Carolina's longest-serving governor (since Jan 2025). First governor in the nation to endorse Trump (2016 — Trump's very first gubernatorial endorsement). Succeeded Nikki Haley when she became UN Ambassador. Presbyterian. NRA A rating. Supports constitutional carry. Appointed by Trump to Homeland Security Advisory Council (April 2025). As AG, defended SC's same-sex marriage ban in court. Signed fetal heartbeat bill. Strong on education parental rights and anti-CRT. Mainstream conservative rather than Christian nationalist — scores low on PCH/CBG.

profile.education: B.A. University of South Carolina; J.D. University of South Carolina School of Law
profile.background: Attorney; U.S. Attorney for South Carolina; state Attorney General (2003-2011); lieutenant governor (2015-2017); became 117th governor in 2017.

candidates.name: Lindsey Graham
candidates.notes: Heritage Action lifetime ~55% — moderate by Republican standards. Air Force JAG Colonel (reserves). Known as John McCain's closest ally in Senate. Proposed 15-week national abortion ban — positioned as moderate pro-life compromise. Voted for 2022 Bipartisan Safer Communities Act — broke with NRA absolutism on red flag laws. Heritage scores dropped 30 points from 114th (30%) as he aligned with Trump, then stabilized 61-66%. Gang of Eight immigration reform member — weakness on immigration from conservative perspective. Southern Baptist but admits attending church ~3 times per year. Single, never married. Evolving Trump relationship: harsh critic in 2016, became ally by 2019.

profile.religion: Southern Baptist (Corinth Baptist Church, Seneca, SC)
profile.education: B.A. University of South Carolina (1977); J.D. University of South Carolina School of Law (1981)
profile.background: Air Force veteran and JAG officer. Former U.S. House member. In Senate since 2003. Former Judiciary Committee chairman. 2016 presidential candidate. Military lawyer.

district_offices.address: 401 W. Evans St., Suite 111, McMillan Federal Building

claims.text: Voted for 2022 Bipartisan Safer Communities Act — broke with NRA absolutism on red flag laws.

candidates.name: Tim Scott
candidates.notes: Heritage Action lifetime ~85% — one of Senate's most conservative members. NRA A grade. Only Black Republican senator. Evangelical Protestant at Seacoast Church (nondenominational megachurch). Says 'I see myself first as a biblical leader.' Fellowship of Christian Athletes helped him discover faith. 2024 presidential candidate — ran on faith and optimism; suspended campaign Nov 2023 and endorsed Trump. Was famously single and celibate — married Mindy Noce in Aug 2024. Appointed to Senate by Nikki Haley in 2013. Proposed Opportunity Zones (included in 2017 tax bill). Despite strong conservative record, does not align with Christian nationalist framing — scores 0 on PCH/CBG due to pluralist optimism and Black evangelical identity.

profile.religion: Evangelical Protestant (Seacoast Church, Charleston — nondenominational evangelical)
profile.education: B.S. Political Science, Charleston Southern University (1988)
profile.background: Former insurance agent and financial advisor. Former Charleston County Council member and U.S. House member. Only Black Republican in Senate. 2024 presidential candidate. Appointed in 2013.

candidates.name: Thomas C. Alexander
candidates.notes: President of the South Carolina Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_South_Carolina_Senate.

profile.leadership_role: President of the South Carolina Senate
